title: Exploring Hyperbolas: A Visual Guide
summary: Dive into the fascinating world of hyperbolas with this educational video where we dissect and understand their unique properties, equations, and real-world applications.

sections.heading: Understanding Hyperbola Equations
sections.body: Hyperbolas are defined by the equation `(x^2/a^2) - (y^2/b^2) = 1` for horizontal hyperbolas or vice versa. In this section, we'll break down how these equations represent two separate curves and explore their symmetries.

sections.heading: Graphical Analysis of Hyperbolas
sections.body: We graph hyperbolas by plotting points that satisfy the equation and connecting them smoothly, revealing a pair of mirror-image curves known as branches. This section will guide you through sketching these intriguing graphs.

sections.heading: Real World Applications of Hyperbolas
sections.body: Hyperbolas are not just abstract shapes; they have practical uses in fields like navigation and astronomy. By understanding their properties, we can see how these mathematical concepts apply to the world around us.

key_facts: The standard equation of a horizontal hyperbola is `(x^2/a^2) - (y^2/b^2) = 1`
Hyperbolas are symmetrical about both axes and the center
